vt_requirements.note: Every acceptance criterion or truth-state-advancing claim must record these four V&T fields.

title: C-RSP Build Contract — TLC Workbench
goal: Bind TLC Workbench productization as a governed dual-surface workbench for research, paper, and product flows.
objective: Transform TLC into a neurodivergent-first operator workbench with TUI and Web UI parity, evidence-backed governance, and explicit release gates for the research-to-paper-to-product lifecycle.
required_outputs: A bound TLC-WORKBENCH module with no I1 contract gap.
A shared application architecture used by both the TUI and Web UI.
An evidence-backed lifecycle covering research, paper, and product outcomes.
Committed V&T artifacts for every truth-state-advancing Workbench claim.

acceptance_criteria.statement: A parent Workbench module is added to registry and binding surfaces with no I1 gap.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Registry and module binding evidence shows TLC-WORKBENCH is governed by this contract.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Child contracts for surfaces, accessibility, lifecycle, and hardening are committed.
acceptance_criteria.verification: All four child contract files exist in markdown and machine-readable JSON form.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Shared app and service architecture exists and is used by both Web UI and TUI.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Code references show lifecycle mutation flows through shared services rather than surface-local mutations.

acceptance_criteria.statement: The canonical lifecycle works end-to-end in both surfaces.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Automated parity or end-to-end evidence demonstrates equivalent governed outcomes in TUI and Web UI.

acceptance_criteria.statement: All truth-state claims for the Workbench are backed by committed V&T.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Evidence references and V&T artifacts exist for every verified or validated claim.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Any implementation that omits required accessibility or parity checks is blocked.
acceptance_criteria.verification: CI or local enforcement emits explicit halts when accessibility or parity artifacts are missing.

halt_conditions.trigger: The parent Workbench contract is missing, superseded without replacement, or not bound to the initiative.
halt_conditions.resolution: Restore or replace the parent contract before continuing implementation.

halt_conditions.trigger: A core flow exists in only one Workbench surface or produces inconsistent governed results.
halt_conditions.resolution: Restore dual-surface parity for the affected core flow before merge or truth-state advancement.

halt_conditions.trigger: Required accessibility or neurodivergent-first controls are absent from the claimed implementation.
halt_conditions.resolution: Add the missing accessible controls and corresponding verification artifacts.

halt_conditions.trigger: The governed research-to-paper-to-product lifecycle is incomplete or not executable end to end.
halt_conditions.resolution: Complete the required lifecycle phases and verify the canonical flow in both surfaces.

halt_conditions.trigger: A Workbench truth-state advancement is claimed without the required evidence and V&T bundle.
halt_conditions.resolution: Attach the required evidence and V&T artifacts or revert the claim.

evidence_requirements.description: Registry or binding evidence proving TLC-WORKBENCH is governed by this parent contract.
Parity or end-to-end evidence covering the canonical lifecycle across both surfaces.
Truth-state evidence showing every verified claim includes the four required V&T fields.
